propositions: Operation certificates are migrated from per-clone symmetric HMAC self-attestations to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures verifiable by any third party against the signing environment's public key.

Each certificate is attributed to its signing environment under namespace rebar.opcert.v1, and a project may optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment pinned out-of-band in .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml (never the auto-pushed tickets branch).

A merge-gate CI lane extends the shipped verify-identity.yaml gate to verify the required-environment certificate over the merged log, failing when a ticket that must carry one lacks a valid certificate.

A trusted rebar-mcp remote-execution path (library-mode HTTP service behind AWS API Gateway SigV4, Ed25519 key in SSM SecureString) must fetch authoritative ticket/code state itself so a developer cannot forge a passing verdict from client-supplied inputs.

Certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position (main commit SHA)} to prevent replay, with key-validity evaluated at the SIGNATURE event's storage anchor to close the era-rollback gap.

The legacy HMAC scheme is removed via expand-contract migration so both operation-cert and authorship signatures are asymmetric, while the default local offline path remains a fast record and never wedges an operation.
